project_overview:
  name: trackgentic
  type: npm library (TypeScript, Bun runtime)
  purpose: Issue tracker designed for AI agents — file-backed, event-sourced, git-friendly
  distribution: npm package with CLI entry point (`trackgentic` command)

  docs_tool: TypeDoc HTML output (configured, generates to docs-html/)

detail_files:

  - path: design-decisions.yaml
    description: Architectural decisions and rationale (why file-backed, why event-sourced, etc.)
  - path: status.yaml
    description: Current project status, implementation roadmap, open questions, risks

specifications:

  files:
    - path: 01-overview.md
      description: Monorepo structure, library package layout, design principles, dependencies, CLI/API relationship
    - path: 02-data-model.md
      description: All TypeScript types, interfaces, file formats, initial file contents
    - path: 03-api-specification.md
      description: Every Tracker method with params, return types, behavior, error handling
    - path: 04-cli-specification.md
      description: Every CLI command with args, flags, auth, output format, error handling
    - path: 05-implementation-phases.md
      description: 7 phases from foundation to polish, with tasks, tests, and exit criteria per phase
    - path: 06-quality-plan.md
      description: Quality plan — now uses Prettier + ESLint (was Biome). Linting, typechecking (strict+), testing (Bun), test organization, documentation (TypeDoc), quality gates
    - path: 07-phase3-auth-users.md
      description: Phase 3 specification — auth module (resolveAuthor), user CRUD (register/list/revoke/regenerate), auth integration into existing methods, CLI user commands
    - path: 07-phase4-comments-spec.md
      description: Phase 4 specification — computeComments function, 4 Tracker comment methods, CLI comment commands, test plan
    - path: 08-phase5-hierarchy-spec.md
      description: Phase 5 specification — childrenOf map management, hierarchy.ts (upward/downward constraints), Tracker create/update integration, reparenting, test plan
    - path: 07-phase6-blockages-spec.md
      description: Phase 6 specification — dependency-manager (bidirectional maps, cycle detection, batch atomicity, impact score), 4 Tracker blockage methods, auto-resolution on status change, CLI blockage commands, test plan
    - path: 08-phase7-polish-spec.md
      description: Phase 7 specification — JSDoc on all public API, error message polish, edge case & resilience testing, package configuration verification
    - path: next-command-spec.md
      description: "`next <user-name>` command — recommend highest-priority unblocked issue for a user. Returns ComputedIssue or NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE. Filters by assignee, actionable statuses (idea/todo/in-progress), no active blockages. Sort: priority ASC → impact DESC → id ASC."

access_boundaries:
  rule: "CTO must NOT read or modify files under packages/library/. All code changes go through subordinates."
  cto_scope: ".agentic/ files, root-level config, specifications, expertise files"
  code_access: "If code-level insight is needed, ask library-developer or library-quality to report — never read source directly."
  enforced: "2026-05-10 — user explicitly removed CTO read permissions on packages/library/"

delegation_workflow:
  mandatory_sequence: |
    After every library-developer delegation that makes code changes, you MUST delegate to library-quality for validation.
    Sequence: library-developer (implement) → library-quality (validate).
    Never skip the quality validation step. This ensures code is clean, tested, and documented.
  timeout_constraint: |
    Bash tool has a 10-minute (600000ms) timeout. Large phases must be split into sub-delegations of ~5-8 minutes each.
    Split strategy for Phase 3: (A) auth.ts + errors.ts, (B) user methods in tracker.ts, (C) auth integration into existing methods + CLI commands, (D) library-quality validation).
    Each sub-task should be scoped to a few files to stay within timeout.
  docs_ownership: |
    library-quality owns documentation tasks: TypeDoc configuration, doc generation, docs scripts.
    When docs need updating or generating, delegate directly to library-quality (not library-developer).
  access_gaps: |
    Resolved: user granted CTO access to .github/ folder. CTO now owns workflow files.
    Root .gitignore is also writable by CTO.
  note: "Self-check and validation steps are enforced via agent system prompts. No need to duplicate in delegation messages."
  library-developer:
    focus: "Focus delegation messages on the implementation task itself. The agent self-checks typecheck+lint+test before completion."
  library-quality:
    focus: "Always pass the same spec file used for implementation so quality can validate alignment with specs, not just code quality. Include the spec path, what changed, and what behaviors to validate. Also handles documentation tasks (TypeDoc, docs generation)."

docs_absorbed:
  - docs/introduction.md — project vision, file structure, issue properties, CLI overview, config
  - docs/hierarchy.md — parent-child tree, childrenOf map, status constraints, auto-promotion
  - docs/commands.md — full CLI reference: init, create, update, list, view, history, comments, blockages
  - docs/issue-blockages.md — dependency index, blockage lifecycle, cycle detection, impact scoring
  - docs/user-auth.md — token-based auth, user registry, auth modes (open/read-only/strict)

  purpose: Issue tracker designed for AI agents — file-backed, event-sourced, git-friendly
  distribution: npm package with CLI entry point (`trackgentic` command)
  stage: Dogfooding active. Agent runner mechanism live and tested — CTO processed 3 `/worktask` invocations for test issue mp5r0cd19j, correctly following status-based workflow (idea → analyze+comment, no code changes). All pending work tracked as trackgentic issues. Current issue chain: cap upward auto-promotion at in-progress (mp9xefucmo, in-progress, 4 children). Standing issues: v0.1.0 release (mp3peanz8b), defensive index validation (mp3pe95w9e), typedoc-plugin-markdown cleanup (mp3pe9lvdq), concurrent writes decision (mp3pe96e9f), CLAUDE.md decision (mp3pe9urb9).
  bun_version: "1.3.11"
  build_tool: tsup (implemented, producing dist/)
  docs_tool: TypeDoc HTML output (configured, generates to docs-html/)

detail_files:
  - path: architecture.yaml
    description: Core data model, file structure, event sourcing, resolution algorithm, index design
  - path: design-decisions.yaml
    description: Architectural decisions and rationale (why file-backed, why event-sourced, etc.)
  - path: status.yaml
    description: Current project status, implementation roadmap, open questions, risks

specifications:
  location: .agentic/specs/
  files:
    - path: 01-overview.md
      description: Monorepo structure, library package layout, design principles, dependencies, CLI/API relationship
    - path: 02-data-model.md
      description: All TypeScript types, interfaces, file formats, initial file contents
    - path: 03-api-specification.md
      description: Every Tracker method with params, return types, behavior, error handling
    - path: 04-cli-specification.md
      description: Every CLI command with args, flags, auth, output format, error handling
    - path: 05-implementation-phases.md
      description: 7 phases from foundation to polish, with tasks, tests, and exit criteria per phase
    - path: 06-quality-plan.md
      description: Quality plan — now uses Prettier + ESLint (was Biome). Linting, typechecking (strict+), testing (Bun), test organization, documentation (TypeDoc), quality gates
    - path: 07-phase3-auth-users.md
      description: Phase 3 specification — auth module (resolveAuthor), user CRUD (register/list/revoke/regenerate), auth integration into existing methods, CLI user commands
    - path: 07-phase4-comments-spec.md
      description: Phase 4 specification — computeComments function, 4 Tracker comment methods, CLI comment commands, test plan
    - path: 08-phase5-hierarchy-spec.md
      description: Phase 5 specification — childrenOf map management, hierarchy.ts (upward/downward constraints), Tracker create/update integration, reparenting, test plan
    - path: 07-phase6-blockages-spec.md
      description: Phase 6 specification — dependency-manager (bidirectional maps, cycle detection, batch atomicity, impact score), 4 Tracker blockage methods, auto-resolution on status change, CLI blockage commands, test plan
    - path: 08-phase7-polish-spec.md
      description: Phase 7 specification — JSDoc on all public API, error message polish, edge case & resilience testing, package configuration verification
    - path: next-command-spec.md
      description: "`next <user-name>` command — recommend highest-priority unblocked issue for a user. Returns ComputedIssue or NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE. Filters by assignee, actionable statuses (idea/todo/in-progress), no active blockages. Sort: priority ASC → impact DESC → id ASC."

access_boundaries:
  rule: "CTO must NOT read or modify files under packages/library/. All code changes go through subordinates."
  cto_scope: ".agentic/ files, root-level config, specifications, expertise files"
  code_access: "If code-level insight is needed, ask library-developer or library-quality to report — never read source directly."
  enforced: "2026-05-10 — user explicitly removed CTO read permissions on packages/library/"

delegation_workflow:
  mandatory_sequence: |
    After every library-developer delegation that makes code changes, you MUST delegate to library-quality for validation.
    Sequence: library-developer (implement) → library-quality (validate).
    Never skip the quality validation step. This ensures code is clean, tested, and documented.
  timeout_constraint: |
    Bash tool has a 10-minute (600000ms) timeout. Large phases must be split into sub-delegations of ~5-8 minutes each.
    Split strategy for Phase 3: (A) auth.ts + errors.ts, (B) user methods in tracker.ts, (C) auth integration into existing methods + CLI commands, (D) library-quality validation).
    Each sub-task should be scoped to a few files to stay within timeout.
  docs_ownership: |
    library-quality owns documentation tasks: TypeDoc configuration, doc generation, docs scripts.
    When docs need updating or generating, delegate directly to library-quality (not library-developer).
  access_gaps: |
    Resolved: user granted CTO access to .github/ folder. CTO now owns workflow files.
    Root .gitignore is also writable by CTO.
  note: "Self-check and validation steps are enforced via agent system prompts. No need to duplicate in delegation messages."
  library-developer:
    focus: "Focus delegation messages on the implementation task itself. The agent self-checks typecheck+lint+test before completion."
  library-quality:
    focus: "Always pass the same spec file used for implementation so quality can validate alignment with specs, not just code quality. Include the spec path, what changed, and what behaviors to validate. Also handles documentation tasks (TypeDoc, docs generation)."

docs_absorbed:
  - docs/introduction.md — project vision, file structure, issue properties, CLI overview, config
  - docs/hierarchy.md — parent-child tree, childrenOf map, status constraints, auto-promotion
  - docs/commands.md — full CLI reference: init, create, update, list, view, history, comments, blockages
  - docs/issue-blockages.md — dependency index, blockage lifecycle, cycle detection, impact scoring
  - docs/user-auth.md — token-based auth, user registry, auth modes (open/read-only/strict)

[
  {
    "id": "mp9xoved4v",
    "author": "javi",
    "content": "## Spec Review: Cap Upward Auto-Promotion at `in-progress` (Quality Perspective)

### Testability Assessment: GOOD

The spec is well-structured for testing. The core change is localized to a single pure function (`computeUpwardPromotions` in hierarchy.ts), which is already tested in isolation (6 tests in hierarchy.test.ts) and via integration (7 tests in tracker-hierarchy.test.ts). The dual call sites in tracker.ts (status change at line 810, reparent at line 779) are both covered by existing tests.

### Missing Acceptance Criteria

1. **Reason string behavior when promotion is capped**: Currently the event reason says `auto-promoted: child advanced to 'done'`. After capping, if the child goes to `done` and the parent is promoted to `in-progress`, what does the reason say? The spec should clarify whether:
   - The reason reflects the **capped status** (`auto-promoted: child advanced to 'in-progress'` — but that's misleading since the child didn't go to in-progress)
   - The reason reflects the **child's actual status** (`auto-promoted: child advanced to 'done'` — but the parent was promoted to in-progress, not done, which is confusing)
   - A new reason format is needed, e.g. `auto-promoted to 'in-progress': child advanced to 'done'` (shows both the target status and the trigger)

2. **Capping when parent is already at `in-progress`**: If child → `done` and parent is already `in-progress`, the capped promotion status is `in-progress` which equals the parent. Should `isStatusAfter(cappedStatus, parentStatus)` return false (no promotion event) or should the parent still get a system event? The spec implies no promotion occurs, but this should be explicit.

3. **Multi-level capping behavior**: Consider a 3-level tree: grandparent (idea) → parent (todo) → child (done). With capping:
   - Parent gets promoted to `in-progress` (capped from done)
   - Grandparent gets promoted to... `in-progress` (since the parent's effective promotion was in-progress)?
   
   The spec says "The function uses `isStatusAfter(childStatus, parentStatus)` to decide whether to promote" — but after capping, what's passed up to the grandparent? The **original child status** (done) or the **capped status** (in-progress)? This is the most critical ambiguity. The implementation note says "use that capped status for both the `isStatusAfter` check and the event content" but the function signature takes `childStatus` as a parameter — is it capped once at the entry point, or does each level re-cap?

### Edge Cases That Need Coverage

1. **Child → `closed` with parent at `todo`**: Closed is past in-progress, so the cap should promote parent to `in-progress` only. Need explicit test.

2. **Child → `done` with parent already at `in-progress`**: Capped status equals parent status. No promotion event should be generated. This is a key regression test.

3. **Child → `done` with parent at `idea`**: Should promote to `in-progress` (not done, not todo). Currently promotes to `done`. Need test for each parent starting status: idea, todo, in-progress, done, closed.

4. **Reparent a `done` child to an `idea` parent**: The reparent call site (tracker.ts:779) also triggers `computeUpwardPromotions`. After the change, the parent should be promoted to `in-progress`, not `done`. The existing test "reparent with upward constraint — parent auto-promoted" (tracker-hierarchy.test.ts:468) currently expects `done` and will break.

5. **Reparent a `closed` child**: Even more extreme — closed is past done, so with capping the parent goes to `in-progress`. No existing test covers reparenting a closed child.

6. **Concurrent upward + downward**: If a parent is closed manually (downward cascade) and then the cascade triggers upward promotion somehow — is this possible? The spec says downward is unchanged, but a test confirming the interaction would be valuable.

7. **Status order boundary**: What happens with statuses that are not in the standard progression? The spec assumes only standard statuses, but a defensive test with the exact STATUS_ORDER array would document this assumption.

### Documentation Requirements

1. **JSDoc on `computeUpwardPromotions`**: The current JSDoc says "Auto-promote parent if child's new status is past parent's status." This needs to be updated to mention the cap: "Auto-promote parent to at most `in-progress` if child's new status is past parent's status. Parents are never auto-promoted to `done` or `closed`."

2. **Event reason documentation**: The `reason` field in system-generated events is user-visible (via `trackgentic history`). The new format should be documented and consistent.

3. **Public API docs**: If `computeUpwardPromotions` is exported (it is — used by tracker.ts), its contract change should be noted in any CHANGELOG or migration guide.

### Test Update Plan (Blast Radius)

**hierarchy.test.ts** — 6 tests in `computeUpwardPromotions` describe block:
- "child ahead of parent returns one promotion" (line 236): Child is `done`, parent is `in-progress`. After capping, promoted status = `in-progress` = parent status → NO promotion event. Test needs to expect empty result, or change parent status to `idea`/`todo`.
- "multi-level: grandchild promotes child promotes parent" (line 252): Both levels currently expect `done`. After capping, both should expect `in-progress`.
- "stops when parent status is already at or past child" (line 272): Parent gets promoted to `done` → capped to `in-progress`. Grandparent is `closed` so stops. Need to update expected promotion status.
- "stops when parent has no parent" (line 285): Parent at `todo`, child `done`. Promoted to `in-progress` (capped). Update expected.
- "findEntry returns null" (line 295): Parent at `idea`, child `done`. Promoted to `in-progress` (capped). Update expected.
- "child behind parent" and "child equal to parent" — these should remain unchanged.

**tracker-hierarchy.test.ts** — 3 upward promotion tests:
- "child → done when parent is in-progress — parent auto-promoted" (line 298): Parent is `in-progress`, child → `done`. After capping, parent stays at `in-progress` (capped status = parent status). This test may need to change parent starting status to `idea` or `todo` to remain meaningful.
- "multi-level: grandchild → done → child promoted → parent promoted" (line 312): Both levels expect `done`. Both should expect `in-progress`.
- "reparent with upward constraint — parent auto-promoted" (line 468): Reparent a `done` child to `todo` parent. Currently expects `done`. Should expect `in-progress`.

**tracker.test.ts** (monolithic — deprecated but still exists): Same 3 tests exist there and need the same updates.

### Additional Test Cases to ADD (not just update)

1. `child → done, parent at in-progress → no promotion event` (the key new behavior)
2. `child → done, parent at idea → promoted to in-progress` (not done)
3. `child → done, parent at todo → promoted to in-progress` (not done)
4. `child → closed, parent at idea → promoted to in-progress` (closed also capped)
5. `child → closed, parent at in-progress → no promotion` (capped = current)
6. `reparent done child to idea parent → promoted to in-progress` (not done)
7. `explicit parent → done still works` (requirement #4 — manual transitions unaffected)

### Summary

The spec is clear in intent and well-scoped. The main risk is:
1. **Ambiguity in reason strings** when the promoted status differs from the child's actual status
2. **Multi-level propagation semantics** — whether the original child status or the capped status propagates upward
3. **Large test blast radius** — 9+ existing tests need updates, and 7+ new tests should be added

Recommendation: Before implementation begins, clarify items #1 and #2 from "Missing Acceptance Criteria" above. Everything else is actionable as-is.",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-17T15:32:32.348Z",
    "editedAt": null
  }
]

1	# Cap Upward Auto-Promotion at `in-progress`
2	
3	**Status: DRAFT**
4	
5	## Summary
6	
7	Currently, when all children of a parent issue reach status `done`, the parent is automatically promoted to `done` via `computeUpwardPromotions`. This prevents the agent managing the parent from creating additional child issues after the current batch completes. We need to cap upward auto-promotion at `in-progress` so parents are never auto-promoted to `done` or `closed` — those transitions must be explicit.
8	
9	## Requirements
10	
11	1. **Auto-promotion stops at `in-progress`**: When a child advances to `in-progress` and its parent is at `idea` or `todo`, the parent is auto-promoted to `in-progress` (existing behavior, unchanged).
12	2. **No auto-promotion to `done`**: When a child advances to `done`, the parent must NOT be auto-promoted to `done`. The parent stays at whatever status it currently has.
13	3. **No auto-promotion to `closed`**: When a child advances to `closed`, the parent must NOT be auto-promoted to `closed`. (Closed parents already exist, but auto-promotion to closed is also removed.)
14	4. **Manual parent transitions still work**: Explicit `tracker.update(parentId, { status: "done" })` must still work, subject to existing hierarchy constraints (children must all be `done`/`closed`).
15	5. **Downward cascading unchanged**: When a parent is explicitly closed, children that are `done` should still be auto-closed (existing behavior).
16	
17	## API / Interface Changes
18

20	
21	The function currently promotes the parent to match the child's status. Change it to cap the promoted status at `in-progress`:
22	
23	```
24	Promoted status = min(childStatus, in-progress)
25	```
26	
27	If the computed promoted status is not after the parent's current status, no promotion occurs.
28	
29	### `STATUS_ORDER` remains unchanged
30	
31	The status order `["idea", "todo", "in-progress", "done", "closed"]` does not change.
32	
33	## Implementation Notes
34	
35	- **File**: `packages/library/src/core/hierarchy.ts` — function `computeUpwardPromotions`
36	- The function uses `isStatusAfter(childStatus, parentStatus)` to decide whether to promote. After computing the effective promotion status (capped at `in-progress`), use that capped status for both the `isStatusAfter` check and the event content.
37	- **Tests**: Multiple existing tests assert that parents ARE auto-promoted to `done`. These must be updated to expect the parent to stay at `in-progress` instead.
38	- **Multi-level tests**: The test "multi-level: grandchild → done → child promoted → parent promoted" (tracker.test.ts ~line 2081) currently expects a cascade to `done` at every level. After this change, no level should reach `done` via auto-promotion.
39	- Key test cases to update in `hierarchy.test.ts`:
40	  - Tests at lines ~240, ~248 asserting `"auto-promoted: child advanced to 'done'"` → should expect no promotion event
41	  - Tests around lines ~277-299 for multi-level promotion to `done`/`closed`
42	- Key test cases to update in `tracker.test.ts`:
43	  - "child → done when parent is in-progress — parent auto-promoted" (~line 2067)
44	  - "multi-level: grandchild → done → child promoted → parent promoted" (~line 2081)
45	
46	## Out of Scope
47	
48	- Changes to downward cascading (parent → children auto-close)
49	- Changes to the `done` guard (preventing parent from going to `done` when children aren't all `done`/`closed`)
50	- Changes to the `isStatusAfter` or `STATUS_ORDER` definitions
51	- CLI changes or new commands

file.content:
# Cap Upward Auto-Promotion at `in-progress`

**Status: DRAFT**

## Summary

Currently, when all children of a parent issue reach status `done`, the parent is automatically promoted to `done` via `computeUpwardPromotions`. This prevents the agent managing the parent from creating additional child issues after the current batch completes. We need to cap upward auto-promotion at `in-progress` so parents are never auto-promoted to `done` or `closed` — those transitions must be explicit.

## Requirements

1. **Auto-promotion stops at `in-progress`**: When a child advances to `in-progress` and its parent is at `idea` or `todo`, the parent is auto-promoted to `in-progress` (existing behavior, unchanged).
2. **No auto-promotion to `done`**: When a child advances to `done`, the parent must NOT be auto-promoted to `done`. The parent stays at whatever status it currently has.
3. **No auto-promotion to `closed`**: When a child advances to `closed`, the parent must NOT be auto-promoted to `closed`. (Closed parents already exist, but auto-promotion to closed is also removed.)
4. **Manual parent transitions still work**: Explicit `tracker.update(parentId, { status: "done" })` must still work, subject to existing hierarchy constraints (children must all be `done`/`closed`).
5. **Downward cascading unchanged**: When a parent is explicitly closed, children that are `done` should still be auto-closed (existing behavior).

## API / Interface Changes

### `computeUpwardPromotions` (hierarchy.ts)

The function currently promotes the parent to match the child's status. Change it to cap the promoted status at `in-progress`:

```
Promoted status = min(childStatus, in-progress)
```

If the computed promoted status is not after the parent's current status, no promotion occurs.

### `STATUS_ORDER` remains unchanged

The status order `["idea", "todo", "in-progress", "done", "closed"]` does not change.

## Implementation Notes

- **File**: `packages/library/src/core/hierarchy.ts` — function `computeUpwardPromotions`
- The function uses `isStatusAfter(childStatus, parentStatus)` to decide whether to promote. After computing the effective promotion status (capped at `in-progress`), use that capped status for both the `isStatusAfter` check and the event content.
- **Tests**: Multiple existing tests assert that parents ARE auto-promoted to `done`. These must be updated to expect the parent to stay at `in-progress` instead.
- **Multi-level tests**: The test "multi-level: grandchild → done → child promoted → parent promoted" (tracker.test.ts ~line 2081) currently expects a cascade to `done` at every level. After this change, no level should reach `done` via auto-promotion.
- Key test cases to update in `hierarchy.test.ts`:
  - Tests at lines ~240, ~248 asserting `"auto-promoted: child advanced to 'done'"` → should expect no promotion event
  - Tests around lines ~277-299 for multi-level promotion to `done`/`closed`
- Key test cases to update in `tracker.test.ts`:
  - "child → done when parent is in-progress — parent auto-promoted" (~line 2067)
  - "multi-level: grandchild → done → child promoted → parent promoted" (~line 2081)

## Out of Scope

- Changes to downward cascading (parent → children auto-close)
- Changes to the `done` guard (preventing parent from going to `done` when children aren't all `done`/`closed`)
- Changes to the `isStatusAfter` or `STATUS_ORDER` definitions
- CLI changes or new commands

content.thinking:
Good. Now I have the full picture. The quality reviewer (library-quality) raised several excellent questions. Let me analyze each:

### Question 1: Reason string behavior when promotion is capped
The current reason is `auto-promoted: child advanced to 'done'`. After capping:
- If the child goes to `done` and the parent is promoted to `in-progress`, the reason could be:
  - `auto-promoted: child advanced to 'in-progress'` — misleading, child didn't go to in-progress
  - `auto-promoted: child advanced to 'done'` — confusing, parent was promoted to in-progress, not done
  - New format: `auto-promoted to 'in-progress': child advanced to 'done'`

My decision: The reason should describe what actually happened. The most accurate format would be `auto-promoted to 'in-progress': child advanced to 'done'`. This clearly communicates both the trigger and the result.

Actually, wait. Let me re-read the spec. Requirement #2 says: "When a child advances to `done`, the parent must NOT be auto-promoted to `done`. The parent stays at whatever status it currently has."

Hmm, that's ambiguous. Does it mean the parent doesn't get promoted AT ALL when the child goes to done? Or does it mean the parent gets promoted but capped at in-progress?

Let me re-read more carefully. Requirement #1 says: "When a child advances to `in-progress` and its parent is at `idea` or `todo`, the parent is auto-promoted to `in-progress` (existing behavior, unchanged)."

And the API section says: `Promoted status = min(childStatus, in-progress)`. So if the child goes to `done`, the promoted status = min(done, in-progress) = in-progress.

So the parent DOES get promoted — just capped at in-progress. The spec is a bit confusing because requirement #2 says "the parent stays at whatever status it currently has" — but that's only true if the parent is already at in-progress or beyond. If the parent is at `idea`, it should be promoted to `in-progress`.

OK, so the spec IS saying: cap the promotion at in-progress. If the child goes to `done` and the parent is at `todo`, the parent gets promoted to `in-progress`.

Now, for the reason string: I think we should use a format like `auto-promoted to 'in-progress': child advanced to 'done'` — it's the most informative. But actually, looking at the current reason format, it's `auto-promoted: child advanced to 'done'`. It doesn't currently include the target status in the reason. So maybe we should keep it simple and just change it to indicate the actual promotion result.

Actually, let me think about this differently. The reason string should answer "why was this event created?" In the current system, the reason tells you what triggered the promotion. After capping, the trigger is still the child advancing, but the result is different.

I think the cleanest approach is:
- `auto-promoted to 'in-progress': child advanced to 'done'` — when the promotion was capped
- `auto-promoted: child advanced to 'in-progress'` — when the promotion is natural (not capped)

Actually, let me look at the existing code to see how the reason is constructed.

### Question 2: Capping when parent is already at `in-progress`
If child → `done` and parent is already `in-progress`, the capped status = `in-progress` = parent status. `isStatusAfter(in-progress, in-progress)` should return false. No promotion event. This is correct behavior.

### Question 3: Multi-level capping behavior
This is the most important question. Consider: grandparent (idea) → parent (todo) → child (done).

The current `computeUpwardPromotions` function iterates upward. For each parent, it takes the child's status and checks if it's after the parent's status.

With capping:
- Parent (todo) gets child's status (done), caps to in-progress, checks isStatusAfter(in-progress, todo) = true → promoted to in-progress
- Now for the grandparent: what's the "child's status"? Is it the original `done` or the capped `in-progress`?

Looking at the spec: "The function uses `isStatusAfter(childStatus, parentStatus)` to decide whether to promote. After computing the effective promotion status (capped at `in-progress`), use that capped status for both the `isStatusAfter` check and the event content."

So the capped status propagates upward. This means:
- Grandparent (idea) gets the parent's NEW status (in-progress), caps to min(in-progress, in-progress) = in-progress, checks isStatusAfter(in-progress, idea) = true → promoted to in-progress

Wait, but actually the function takes `childStatus` as a parameter. The question is: when iterating upward, does it pass the original child status or the capped status?

Looking at the implementation notes: "After computing the effective promotion status (capped at `in-progress`), use that capped status for both the `isStatusAfter` check and the event content."

I think the intent is: at each level, take the incoming child status, cap it at in-progress, and then propagate THAT capped status upward to the next level. So the capped status propagates.

This makes sense: the whole point is that auto-promotion never reaches done/closed, so the grandparent should also never auto-promote past in-progress.

Actually, there's a subtlety. Let me think about this more carefully.

The function `computeUpwardPromotions` takes a child status and walks up the tree. At each level:
1. Cap the child status at in-progress
2. If capped status > parent status, promote parent to capped status
3. Pass the child's status to the next level up

But what gets passed up? The original child status (before capping) or the capped status?

If the original `done` status gets passed up, then each level independently caps it. This means:
- Level 1: child(done) → parent(todo): cap done to in-progress → promote to in-progress. Pass done up.
- Level 2: child(done) → grandparent(idea): cap done to in-progress → promote to in-progress. 

If the capped status gets passed up:
- Level 1: child(done) → parent(todo): cap done to in-progress → promote to in-progress. Pass in-progress up.
- Level 2: child(in-progress) → grandparent(idea): cap in-progress to in-progress → promote to in-progress.

Either way, the result is the same! Because capping is idempotent — `min(anything, in-progress)` always produces the same result regardless of whether you cap once or at every level.

Wait, no. Consider: grandparent(idea) → parent(todo) → child(closed).
- Original approach: At level 1, cap closed to in-progress. At level 2, cap closed to in-progress. Same result.
- Capped propagation: At level 1, cap closed to in-progress, pass in-progress up. At level 2, cap in-progress to in-progress. Same result.

Yes, both approaches produce the same result because `min(min(x, ip), ip) = min(x, ip)`.

But wait — what about a different scenario? What if the parent was explicitly promoted to done? Then:
- grandparent(idea) → parent(done) → child(done)

In the current system, the child is done, parent is done (child's status not after parent), so no promotion. Grandparent doesn't get promoted.

With capping, cap done to in-progress. isStatusAfter(in-progress, done) = false. No promotion at level 1. Nothing propagates to grandparent.

OK so the semantics don't actually differ between the two approaches for the cap-at-in-progress case. The capping is always applied before the isStatusAfter check, so it doesn't matter whether the original or capped status propagates.

Actually wait, I need to look at the actual function. Let me read the current implementation.

Actually, I should just address the reviewer's concerns by updating the spec. Let me think about what decisions to make:

1. **Reason strings**: Use format `auto-promoted to 'in-progress': child advanced to 'done'` when capped, keep existing format when not capped. Actually, this is getting complex. Let me simplify: just use `auto-promoted to '<target-status>': child advanced to '<child-status>'`. This works for both capped and uncapped cases.

Actually wait, let me look at how the reason is currently used in the code.

2. **Parent already at in-progress**: Explicitly state: no promotion event generated when capped status equals parent status.

3. **Multi-level**: The capped status propagates upward. Since `min(min(x, ip), ip) = min(x, ip)`, the result is the same regardless, but for clarity, each level applies the cap independently.

Let me now read the current implementation of computeUpwardPromotions to understand the reason string format.

# Cap Upward Auto-Promotion at `in-progress`

**Status: DRAFT**

## Summary

Currently, when all children of a parent issue reach status `done`, the parent is automatically promoted to `done` via `computeUpwardPromotions`. This prevents the agent managing the parent from creating additional child issues after the current batch completes. We need to cap upward auto-promotion at `in-progress` so parents are never auto-promoted to `done` or `closed` — those transitions must be explicit.

## Requirements

1. **Auto-promotion stops at `in-progress`**: When a child advances to `in-progress` and its parent is at `idea` or `todo`, the parent is auto-promoted to `in-progress` (existing behavior, unchanged).
2. **No auto-promotion to `done`**: When a child advances to `done`, the parent must NOT be auto-promoted to `done`. The parent stays at whatever status it currently has.
3. **No auto-promotion to `closed`**: When a child advances to `closed`, the parent must NOT be auto-promoted to `closed`. (Closed parents already exist, but auto-promotion to closed is also removed.)
4. **Manual parent transitions still work**: Explicit `tracker.update(parentId, { status: "done" })` must still work, subject to existing hierarchy constraints (children must all be `done`/`closed`).
5. **Downward cascading unchanged**: When a parent is explicitly closed, children that are `done` should still be auto-closed (existing behavior).

## API / Interface Changes

### `computeUpwardPromotions` (hierarchy.ts)

The function currently promotes the parent to match the child's status. Change it to cap the promoted status at `in-progress`:

```
Promoted status = min(childStatus, in-progress)
```

If the computed promoted status is not after the parent's current status, no promotion occurs.

### `STATUS_ORDER` remains unchanged

The status order `["idea", "todo", "in-progress", "done", "closed"]` does not change.

## Implementation Notes

- **File**: `packages/library/src/core/hierarchy.ts` — function `computeUpwardPromotions`
- The function uses `isStatusAfter(childStatus, parentStatus)` to decide whether to promote. After computing the effective promotion status (capped at `in-progress`), use that capped status for both the `isStatusAfter` check and the event content.
- **Tests**: Multiple existing tests assert that parents ARE auto-promoted to `done`. These must be updated to expect the parent to stay at `in-progress` instead.
- **Multi-level tests**: The test "multi-level: grandchild → done → child promoted → parent promoted" (tracker.test.ts ~line 2081) currently expects a cascade to `done` at every level. After this change, no level should reach `done` via auto-promotion.
- Key test cases to update in `hierarchy.test.ts`:
  - Tests at lines ~240, ~248 asserting `"auto-promoted: child advanced to 'done'"` → should expect no promotion event
  - Tests around lines ~277-299 for multi-level promotion to `done`/`closed`
- Key test cases to update in `tracker.test.ts`:
  - "child → done when parent is in-progress — parent auto-promoted" (~line 2067)
  - "multi-level: grandchild → done → child promoted → parent promoted" (~line 2081)

## Out of Scope

- Changes to downward cascading (parent → children auto-close)
- Changes to the `done` guard (preventing parent from going to `done` when children aren't all `done`/`closed`)
- Changes to the `isStatusAfter` or `STATUS_ORDER` definitions
- CLI changes or new commands

## Out of Scope

- CLI changes or new commands
input.new_string:
## Clarifications (post-review)

### 1. Reason string format

The event `reason` field should reflect both the target status and the trigger. Use this format:

```
auto-promoted to '<target-status>': child advanced to '<child-status>'
```

Examples:
- Child → `done`, parent at `todo` → `auto-promoted to 'in-progress': child advanced to 'done'`
- Child → `in-progress`, parent at `idea` → `auto-promoted to 'in-progress': child advanced to 'in-progress'`
- Child → `closed`, parent at `idea` → `auto-promoted to 'in-progress': child advanced to 'closed'`

This replaces the current format `auto-promoted: child advanced to '<status>'` with a more explicit version.

### 2. No promotion when capped status equals parent status

If the computed capped status equals the parent's current status, `isStatusAfter(cappedStatus, parentStatus)` returns `false` and **no promotion event is generated**. This is the key behavioral change:

- Child → `done`, parent at `in-progress` → capped = `in-progress` = parent → **no promotion, no event**.
- Child → `done`, parent at `done` → capped = `in-progress` < parent → **no promotion, no event**.
- Child → `done`, parent at `idea` → capped = `in-progress` > parent → **promote to `in-progress`**.

### 3. Multi-level propagation: each level applies cap independently

At each level of the tree walk, the function takes the **original child status** as input, applies the cap (`min(childStatus, in-progress)`), then checks against the parent. The original child status propagates upward (not the capped status).

Since `min(min(x, ip), ip) = min(x, ip)`, the mathematical result is identical either way. But for clarity: the function receives the **triggering child's actual status** at every level, and each level applies the cap independently.

Example: grandparent (idea) → parent (todo) → child (done):
- Level 1: cap(`done`) = `in-progress`. isStatusAfter(`in-progress`, `todo`) = true → promote parent to `in-progress`.
- Level 2: cap(`done`) = `in-progress`. isStatusAfter(`in-progress`, `idea`) = true → promote grandparent to `in-progress`.

### 4. Additional test cases (required)

Beyond updating existing tests, the following new test cases must be added:

| Test | Parent status | Child becomes | Expected |
|------|--------------|---------------|----------|
| Cap prevents done promotion | `todo` | `done` | Parent → `in-progress` (not `done`) |
| Cap with parent at in-progress | `in-progress` | `done` | No promotion event |
| Cap with parent at idea | `idea` | `done` | Parent → `in-progress` |
| Closed child also capped | `idea` | `closed` | Parent → `in-progress` (not `closed`) |
| Closed child, parent at in-progress | `in-progress` | `closed` | No promotion |
| Reparent done child to idea parent | `idea` | (already `done`) | Parent → `in-progress` (not `done`) |
| Explicit parent → done still works | any | any | `tracker.update(parentId, { status: "done" })` succeeds if children all `done`/`closed` |

## Out of Scope

- Changes to downward cascading (parent → children auto-close)
- Changes to the `done` guard (preventing parent from going to `done` when children aren't all `done`/`closed`)
- Changes to the `isStatusAfter` or `STATUS_ORDER` definitions
- CLI changes or new commands

1	# Cap Upward Auto-Promotion at `in-progress`
2	
3	**Status: REVIEWED — ready for implementation**
4	
5	## Summary
6	
7	Currently, when all children of a parent issue reach status `done`, the parent is automatically promoted to `done` via `computeUpwardPromotions`. This prevents the agent managing the parent from creating additional child issues after the current batch completes. We need to cap upward auto-promotion at `in-progress` so parents are never auto-promoted to `done` or `closed` — those transitions must be explicit.
8	
9	## Requirements
10	
11	1. **Auto-promotion stops at `in-progress`**: When a child advances to `in-progress` and its parent is at `idea` or `todo`, the parent is auto-promoted to `in-progress` (existing behavior, unchanged).
12	2. **No auto-promotion to `done`**: When a child advances to `done`, the parent must NOT be auto-promoted to `done`. The parent stays at whatever status it currently has.
13	3. **No auto-promotion to `closed`**: When a child advances to `closed`, the parent must NOT be auto-promoted to `closed`. (Closed parents already exist, but auto-promotion to closed is also removed.)
14	4. **Manual parent transitions still work**: Explicit `tracker.update(parentId, { status: "done" })` must still work, subject to existing hierarchy constraints (children must all be `done`/`closed`).
15	5. **Downward cascading unchanged**: When a parent is explicitly closed, children that are `done` should still be auto-closed (existing behavior).
16	
17	## API / Interface Changes
18	
19	### `computeUpwardPromotions` (hierarchy.ts)
20	
21	The function currently promotes the parent to match the child's status. Change it to cap the promoted status at `in-progress`:
22	
23	```
24	Promoted status = min(childStatus, in-progress)
25	```
26	
27	If the computed promoted status is not after the parent's current status, no promotion occurs.
28	
29	### `STATUS_ORDER` remains unchanged
30	
31	The status order `["idea", "todo", "in-progress", "done", "closed"]` does not change.
32	
33	## Implementation Notes
34	
35	- **File**: `packages/library/src/core/hierarchy.ts` — function `computeUpwardPromotions`
36	- The function uses `isStatusAfter(childStatus, parentStatus)` to decide whether to promote. After computing the effective promotion status (capped at `in-progress`), use that capped status for both the `isStatusAfter` check and the event content.
37	- **Tests**: Multiple existing tests assert that parents ARE auto-promoted to `done`. These must be updated to expect the parent to stay at `in-progress` instead.
38	- **Multi-level tests**: The test "multi-level: grandchild → done → child promoted → parent promoted" (tracker.test.ts ~line 2081) currently expects a cascade to `done` at every level. After this change, no level should reach `done` via auto-promotion.
39	- Key test cases to update in `hierarchy.test.ts`:
40	  - Tests at lines ~240, ~248 asserting `"auto-promoted: child advanced to 'done'"` → should expect no promotion event
41	  - Tests around lines ~277-299 for multi-level promotion to `done`/`closed`
42	- Key test cases to update in `tracker.test.ts`:
43	  - "child → done when parent is in-progress — parent auto-promoted" (~line 2067)
44	  - "multi-level: grandchild → done → child promoted → parent promoted" (~line 2081)
45	
46	## Clarifications (post-review)
47	
48	### 1. Reason string format
49	
50	The event `reason` field should reflect both the target status and the trigger. Use this format:
51	
52	```
53	auto-promoted to '<target-status>': child advanced to '<child-status>'
54	```
55	
56	Examples:
57	- Child → `done`, parent at `todo` → `auto-promoted to 'in-progress': child advanced to 'done'`
58	- Child → `in-progress`, parent at `idea` → `auto-promoted to 'in-progress': child advanced to 'in-progress'`
59	- Child → `closed`, parent at `idea` → `auto-promoted to 'in-progress': child advanced to 'closed'`
60	
61	This replaces the current format `auto-promoted: child advanced to '<status>'` with a more explicit version.
62	
63	### 2. No promotion when capped status equals parent status
64	
65	If the computed capped status equals the parent's current status, `isStatusAfter(cappedStatus, parentStatus)` returns `false` and **no promotion event is generated**. This is the key behavioral change:
66	
67	- Child → `done`, parent at `in-progress` → capped = `in-progress` = parent → **no promotion, no event**.
68	- Child → `done`, parent at `done` → capped = `in-progress` < parent → **no promotion, no event**.
69	- Child → `done`, parent at `idea` → capped = `in-progress` > parent → **promote to `in-progress`**.
70	
71	### 3. Multi-level propagation: each level applies cap independently
72	
73	At each level of the tree walk, the function takes the **original child status** as input, applies the cap (`min(childStatus, in-progress)`), then checks against the parent. The original child status propagates upward (not the capped status).
74	
75	Since `min(min(x, ip), ip) = min(x, ip)`, the mathematical result is identical either way. But for clarity: the function receives the **triggering child's actual status** at every level, and each level applies the cap independently.
76	
77	Example: grandparent (idea) → parent (todo) → child (done):
78	- Level 1: cap(`done`) = `in-progress`. isStatusAfter(`in-progress`, `todo`) = true → promote parent to `in-progress`.
79	- Level 2: cap(`done`) = `in-progress`. isStatusAfter(`in-progress`, `idea`) = true → promote grandparent to `in-progress`.
80	
81	### 4. Additional test cases (required)
82	
83	Beyond updating existing tests, the following new test cases must be added:
84	
85	| Test | Parent status | Child becomes | Expected |
86	|------|--------------|---------------|----------|
87	| Cap prevents done promotion | `todo` | `done` | Parent → `in-progress` (not `done`) |
88	| Cap with parent at in-progress | `in-progress` | `done` | No promotion event |
89	| Cap with parent at idea | `idea` | `done` | Parent → `in-progress` |
90	| Closed child also capped | `idea` | `closed` | Parent → `in-progress` (not `closed`) |
91	| Closed child, parent at in-progress | `in-progress` | `closed` | No promotion |
92	| Reparent done child to idea parent | `idea` | (already `done`) | Parent → `in-progress` (not `done`) |
93	| Explicit parent → done still works | any | any | `tracker.update(parentId, { status: "done" })` succeeds if children all `done`/`closed` |
94	
95	## Out of Scope
96	
97	- Changes to downward cascading (parent → children auto-close)
98	- Changes to the `done` guard (preventing parent from going to `done` when children aren't all `done`/`closed`)
99	- Changes to the `isStatusAfter` or `STATUS_ORDER` definitions
100	- CLI changes or new commands

file.content:
# Cap Upward Auto-Promotion at `in-progress`

**Status: REVIEWED — ready for implementation**

## Summary

Currently, when all children of a parent issue reach status `done`, the parent is automatically promoted to `done` via `computeUpwardPromotions`. This prevents the agent managing the parent from creating additional child issues after the current batch completes. We need to cap upward auto-promotion at `in-progress` so parents are never auto-promoted to `done` or `closed` — those transitions must be explicit.

## Requirements

1. **Auto-promotion stops at `in-progress`**: When a child advances to `in-progress` and its parent is at `idea` or `todo`, the parent is auto-promoted to `in-progress` (existing behavior, unchanged).
2. **No auto-promotion to `done`**: When a child advances to `done`, the parent must NOT be auto-promoted to `done`. The parent stays at whatever status it currently has.
3. **No auto-promotion to `closed`**: When a child advances to `closed`, the parent must NOT be auto-promoted to `closed`. (Closed parents already exist, but auto-promotion to closed is also removed.)
4. **Manual parent transitions still work**: Explicit `tracker.update(parentId, { status: "done" })` must still work, subject to existing hierarchy constraints (children must all be `done`/`closed`).
5. **Downward cascading unchanged**: When a parent is explicitly closed, children that are `done` should still be auto-closed (existing behavior).

## API / Interface Changes

### `computeUpwardPromotions` (hierarchy.ts)

The function currently promotes the parent to match the child's status. Change it to cap the promoted status at `in-progress`:

```
Promoted status = min(childStatus, in-progress)
```

If the computed promoted status is not after the parent's current status, no promotion occurs.

### `STATUS_ORDER` remains unchanged

The status order `["idea", "todo", "in-progress", "done", "closed"]` does not change.

## Implementation Notes

- **File**: `packages/library/src/core/hierarchy.ts` — function `computeUpwardPromotions`
- The function uses `isStatusAfter(childStatus, parentStatus)` to decide whether to promote. After computing the effective promotion status (capped at `in-progress`), use that capped status for both the `isStatusAfter` check and the event content.
- **Tests**: Multiple existing tests assert that parents ARE auto-promoted to `done`. These must be updated to expect the parent to stay at `in-progress` instead.
- **Multi-level tests**: The test "multi-level: grandchild → done → child promoted → parent promoted" (tracker.test.ts ~line 2081) currently expects a cascade to `done` at every level. After this change, no level should reach `done` via auto-promotion.
- Key test cases to update in `hierarchy.test.ts`:
  - Tests at lines ~240, ~248 asserting `"auto-promoted: child advanced to 'done'"` → should expect no promotion event
  - Tests around lines ~277-299 for multi-level promotion to `done`/`closed`
- Key test cases to update in `tracker.test.ts`:
  - "child → done when parent is in-progress — parent auto-promoted" (~line 2067)
  - "multi-level: grandchild → done → child promoted → parent promoted" (~line 2081)

## Clarifications (post-review)

### 1. Reason string format

The event `reason` field should reflect both the target status and the trigger. Use this format:

```
auto-promoted to '<target-status>': child advanced to '<child-status>'
```

Examples:
- Child → `done`, parent at `todo` → `auto-promoted to 'in-progress': child advanced to 'done'`
- Child → `in-progress`, parent at `idea` → `auto-promoted to 'in-progress': child advanced to 'in-progress'`
- Child → `closed`, parent at `idea` → `auto-promoted to 'in-progress': child advanced to 'closed'`

This replaces the current format `auto-promoted: child advanced to '<status>'` with a more explicit version.

### 2. No promotion when capped status equals parent status

If the computed capped status equals the parent's current status, `isStatusAfter(cappedStatus, parentStatus)` returns `false` and **no promotion event is generated**. This is the key behavioral change:

- Child → `done`, parent at `in-progress` → capped = `in-progress` = parent → **no promotion, no event**.
- Child → `done`, parent at `done` → capped = `in-progress` < parent → **no promotion, no event**.
- Child → `done`, parent at `idea` → capped = `in-progress` > parent → **promote to `in-progress`**.

### 3. Multi-level propagation: each level applies cap independently

At each level of the tree walk, the function takes the **original child status** as input, applies the cap (`min(childStatus, in-progress)`), then checks against the parent. The original child status propagates upward (not the capped status).

Since `min(min(x, ip), ip) = min(x, ip)`, the mathematical result is identical either way. But for clarity: the function receives the **triggering child's actual status** at every level, and each level applies the cap independently.

Example: grandparent (idea) → parent (todo) → child (done):
- Level 1: cap(`done`) = `in-progress`. isStatusAfter(`in-progress`, `todo`) = true → promote parent to `in-progress`.
- Level 2: cap(`done`) = `in-progress`. isStatusAfter(`in-progress`, `idea`) = true → promote grandparent to `in-progress`.

### 4. Additional test cases (required)

Beyond updating existing tests, the following new test cases must be added:

| Test | Parent status | Child becomes | Expected |
|------|--------------|---------------|----------|
| Cap prevents done promotion | `todo` | `done` | Parent → `in-progress` (not `done`) |
| Cap with parent at in-progress | `in-progress` | `done` | No promotion event |
| Cap with parent at idea | `idea` | `done` | Parent → `in-progress` |
| Closed child also capped | `idea` | `closed` | Parent → `in-progress` (not `closed`) |
| Closed child, parent at in-progress | `in-progress` | `closed` | No promotion |
| Reparent done child to idea parent | `idea` | (already `done`) | Parent → `in-progress` (not `done`) |
| Explicit parent → done still works | any | any | `tracker.update(parentId, { status: "done" })` succeeds if children all `done`/`closed` |

## Out of Scope

- Changes to downward cascading (parent → children auto-close)
- Changes to the `done` guard (preventing parent from going to `done` when children aren't all `done`/`closed`)
- Changes to the `isStatusAfter` or `STATUS_ORDER` definitions
- CLI changes or new commands

file.content:
# CTO Expertise Index
# Agent: cto
# Domain: Project architecture, technical specifications, development alignment

hierarchy:
  manager: none — top-level agent
  subordinates:
    active:
      - library-developer (implementation)
      - library-quality (testing, documentation, quality gates)

expertise_status: "Dogfooding active. `next <user-name>` command implemented (mp3rc7femf, code complete). 569 tests, 1997 assertions, 100% tracker.ts line coverage. New work in flight: cap upward auto-promotion at in-progress — parents never auto-promote to done/closed (parent mp9xefucmo, 2 reviews + implement + validate children). Spec at .agentic/specs/cap-upward-promotion-spec.md (DRAFT). Pending issues: v0.1.0 release (mp3peanz8b), defensive index validation (mp3pe95w9e), typedoc-plugin-markdown cleanup (mp3pe9lvdq), concurrent writes decision (mp3pe96e9f), CLAUDE.md decision (mp3pe9urb9)."

project_overview:
  name: trackgentic
  type: npm library (TypeScript, Bun runtime)
  purpose: Issue tracker designed for AI agents — file-backed, event-sourced, git-friendly
  distribution: npm package with CLI entry point (`trackgentic` command)
  stage: Dogfooding active. Agent runner mechanism live and tested — CTO processed 3 `/worktask` invocations for test issue mp5r0cd19j, correctly following status-based workflow (idea → analyze+comment, no code changes). All pending work tracked as trackgentic issues. Current issue chain: cap upward auto-promotion at in-progress (mp9xefucmo, in-progress, 4 children). Standing issues: v0.1.0 release (mp3peanz8b), defensive index validation (mp3pe95w9e), typedoc-plugin-markdown cleanup (mp3pe9lvdq), concurrent writes decision (mp3pe96e9f), CLAUDE.md decision (mp3pe9urb9).
  bun_version: "1.3.11"
  build_tool: tsup (implemented, producing dist/)
  docs_tool: TypeDoc HTML output (configured, generates to docs-html/)

detail_files:
  - path: architecture.yaml
    description: Core data model, file structure, event sourcing, resolution algorithm, index design
  - path: design-decisions.yaml
    description: Architectural decisions and rationale (why file-backed, why event-sourced, etc.)
  - path: status.yaml
    description: Current project status, implementation roadmap, open questions, risks

specifications:
  location: .agentic/specs/
  files:
    - path: 01-overview.md
      description: Monorepo structure, library package layout, design principles, dependencies, CLI/API relationship
    - path: 02-data-model.md
      description: All TypeScript types, interfaces, file formats, initial file contents
    - path: 03-api-specification.md
      description: Every Tracker method with params, return types, behavior, error handling
    - path: 04-cli-specification.md
      description: Every CLI command with args, flags, auth, output format, error handling
    - path: 05-implementation-phases.md
      description: 7 phases from foundation to polish, with tasks, tests, and exit criteria per phase
    - path: 06-quality-plan.md
      description: Quality plan — now uses Prettier + ESLint (was Biome). Linting, typechecking (strict+), testing (Bun), test organization, documentation (TypeDoc), quality gates
    - path: 07-phase3-auth-users.md
      description: Phase 3 specification — auth module (resolveAuthor), user CRUD (register/list/revoke/regenerate), auth integration into existing methods, CLI user commands
    - path: 07-phase4-comments-spec.md
      description: Phase 4 specification — computeComments function, 4 Tracker comment methods, CLI comment commands, test plan
    - path: 08-phase5-hierarchy-spec.md
      description: Phase 5 specification — childrenOf map management, hierarchy.ts (upward/downward constraints), Tracker create/update integration, reparenting, test plan
    - path: 07-phase6-blockages-spec.md
      description: Phase 6 specification — dependency-manager (bidirectional maps, cycle detection, batch atomicity, impact score), 4 Tracker blockage methods, auto-resolution on status change, CLI blockage commands, test plan
    - path: 08-phase7-polish-spec.md
      description: Phase 7 specification — JSDoc on all public API, error message polish, edge case & resilience testing, package configuration verification
    - path: next-command-spec.md
      description: "`next <user-name>` command — recommend highest-priority unblocked issue for a user. Returns ComputedIssue or NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE. Filters by assignee, actionable statuses (idea/todo/in-progress), no active blockages. Sort: priority ASC → impact DESC → id ASC."

access_boundaries:
  rule: "CTO must NOT read or modify files under packages/library/. All code changes go through subordinates."
  cto_scope: ".agentic/ files, root-level config, specifications, expertise files"
  code_access: "If code-level insight is needed, ask library-developer or library-quality to report — never read source directly."
  enforced: "2026-05-10 — user explicitly removed CTO read permissions on packages/library/"

delegation_workflow:
  mandatory_sequence: |
    After every library-developer delegation that makes code changes, you MUST delegate to library-quality for validation.
    Sequence: library-developer (implement) → library-quality (validate).
    Never skip the quality validation step. This ensures code is clean, tested, and documented.
  timeout_constraint: |
    Bash tool has a 10-minute (600000ms) timeout. Large phases must be split into sub-delegations of ~5-8 minutes each.
    Split strategy for Phase 3: (A) auth.ts + errors.ts, (B) user methods in tracker.ts, (C) auth integration into existing methods + CLI commands, (D) library-quality validation).
    Each sub-task should be scoped to a few files to stay within timeout.
  docs_ownership: |
    library-quality owns documentation tasks: TypeDoc configuration, doc generation, docs scripts.
    When docs need updating or generating, delegate directly to library-quality (not library-developer).
  access_gaps: |
    Resolved: user granted CTO access to .github/ folder. CTO now owns workflow files.
    Root .gitignore is also writable by CTO.
  note: "Self-check and validation steps are enforced via agent system prompts. No need to duplicate in delegation messages."
  library-developer:
    focus: "Focus delegation messages on the implementation task itself. The agent self-checks typecheck+lint+test before completion."
  library-quality:
    focus: "Always pass the same spec file used for implementation so quality can validate alignment with specs, not just code quality. Include the spec path, what changed, and what behaviors to validate. Also handles documentation tasks (TypeDoc, docs generation)."

docs_absorbed:
  - docs/introduction.md — project vision, file structure, issue properties, CLI overview, config
  - docs/hierarchy.md — parent-child tree, childrenOf map, status constraints, auto-promotion
  - docs/commands.md — full CLI reference: init, create, update, list, view, history, comments, blockages
  - docs/issue-blockages.md — dependency index, blockage lifecycle, cycle detection, impact scoring
  - docs/user-auth.md — token-based auth, user registry, auth modes (open/read-only/strict)

session_log:
  - session: 1
    date: "2026-05-10"
    summary: "Read all docs, built full understanding of architecture. Could not write expertise files (missing Write/Edit tools). Requested tool update."
  - session: 2
    date: "2026-05-10"
    summary: "Created all 4 expertise files after Write/Edit tools were added. Validated YAML. Expertise fully initialized."

    date: "2026-05-10"
    summary: "Confirmed expertise is current. No new knowledge gained this session — existing files cover all project docs and architecture."
  - session: 4
    date: "2026-05-10"
    summary: "Generated full library specifications (5 files in .agentic/specs/). Resolved OQ-001 (specs location) and OQ-002 (programmatic API). Updated expertise and status. Ready to delegate to library-developer."
  - session: 5
    date: "2026-05-10"
    summary: "User requested monorepo creation. Attempted to create root package.json and packages/library/ files but hit read-only access restriction. CTO agent can write .agentic/ files but not repo source files. Awaiting permission update or alternative approach."
  - session: 6
    date: "2026-05-10"
    summary: "User updated permissions. Root-level ./* write works. Discovered packages/** subdirectory is not covered — Bash mkdir and file writes to packages/library/ are blocked. Identified needed access rule: 'packages/** with read+write'. Awaiting user to add it."
  - session: 7
    date: "2026-05-10"
    summary: "User created packages/library/ folder. Attempted monorepo initialization but still blocked — ./* access rule only covers root-level files, not packages/** subdirectories. Requested user add 'packages/** with [read, write]' access rule."
  - session: 8
    date: "2026-05-10"
    summary: "User granted packages/** access. Initialized monorepo: root package.json, packages/library/ with package.json/tsconfig.json/bunfig.toml, full src/ and tests/ directory tree. Initially overstepped by writing type source files — user corrected to scaffold only. Deleted all source files, kept config only. Ran bun install successfully."
  - session: 9
    date: "2026-05-10"
    summary: "User hired library-developer subordinate. CTO permissions removed from packages/library. Delegated Phase 1 (Foundation) to library-developer. Phase 1 completed successfully: all types, core modules (errors, id, resolution, file-io, tracker.init), CLI runner with init command, barrel exports. 18 tests, 432 assertions, typecheck clean. Ready for Phase 2."
  - session: 10
    date: "2026-05-10"
    summary: "User hired library-quality subordinate. Created quality plan specification (06-quality-plan.md): Biome for linting, strict TypeScript with extra flags, Bun test runner with coverage thresholds, test file organization (core/cli/integration/helpers), code review checklist, TypeDoc for documentation with typedoc-plugin-markdown. Defined documentation update process triggered by code changes. Updated expertise index with new subordinate."
  - session: 11
    date: "2026-05-10"

# CTO Expertise Index
# Agent: cto
# Domain: Project architecture, technical specifications, development alignment

hierarchy:
  manager: none — top-level agent
  subordinates:
    active:
      - library-developer (implementation)
      - library-quality (testing, documentation, quality gates)

expertise_status: "Dogfooding active. `next <user-name>` command implemented (mp3rc7femf, code complete). 569 tests, 1997 assertions, 100% tracker.ts line coverage. New work in flight: cap upward auto-promotion at in-progress — parents never auto-promote to done/closed (parent mp9xefucmo, 2 reviews + implement + validate children). Spec at .agentic/specs/cap-upward-promotion-spec.md (DRAFT). Pending issues: v0.1.0 release (mp3peanz8b), defensive index validation (mp3pe95w9e), typedoc-plugin-markdown cleanup (mp3pe9lvdq), concurrent writes decision (mp3pe96e9f), CLAUDE.md decision (mp3pe9urb9)."

project_overview:
  name: trackgentic
  type: npm library (TypeScript, Bun runtime)
  purpose: Issue tracker designed for AI agents — file-backed, event-sourced, git-friendly
  distribution: npm package with CLI entry point (`trackgentic` command)
  stage: Dogfooding active. Agent runner mechanism live and tested — CTO processed 3 `/worktask` invocations for test issue mp5r0cd19j, correctly following status-based workflow (idea → analyze+comment, no code changes). All pending work tracked as trackgentic issues. Current issue chain: cap upward auto-promotion at in-progress (mp9xefucmo, in-progress, 4 children). Standing issues: v0.1.0 release (mp3peanz8b), defensive index validation (mp3pe95w9e), typedoc-plugin-markdown cleanup (mp3pe9lvdq), concurrent writes decision (mp3pe96e9f), CLAUDE.md decision (mp3pe9urb9).
  bun_version: "1.3.11"
  build_tool: tsup (implemented, producing dist/)
  docs_tool: TypeDoc HTML output (configured, generates to docs-html/)

detail_files:
  - path: architecture.yaml
    description: Core data model, file structure, event sourcing, resolution algorithm, index design
  - path: design-decisions.yaml
    description: Architectural decisions and rationale (why file-backed, why event-sourced, etc.)
  - path: status.yaml
    description: Current project status, implementation roadmap, open questions, risks

specifications:
  location: .agentic/specs/
  files:
    - path: 01-overview.md
      description: Monorepo structure, library package layout, design principles, dependencies, CLI/API relationship
    - path: 02-data-model.md
      description: All TypeScript types, interfaces, file formats, initial file contents
    - path: 03-api-specification.md
      description: Every Tracker method with params, return types, behavior, error handling
    - path: 04-cli-specification.md
      description: Every CLI command with args, flags, auth, output format, error handling
    - path: 05-implementation-phases.md
      description: 7 phases from foundation to polish, with tasks, tests, and exit criteria per phase
    - path: 06-quality-plan.md
      description: Quality plan — now uses Prettier + ESLint (was Biome). Linting, typechecking (strict+), testing (Bun), test organization, documentation (TypeDoc), quality gates
    - path: 07-phase3-auth-users.md
      description: Phase 3 specification — auth module (resolveAuthor), user CRUD (register/list/revoke/regenerate), auth integration into existing methods, CLI user commands
    - path: 07-phase4-comments-spec.md
      description: Phase 4 specification — computeComments function, 4 Tracker comment methods, CLI comment commands, test plan
    - path: 08-phase5-hierarchy-spec.md
      description: Phase 5 specification — childrenOf map management, hierarchy.ts (upward/downward constraints), Tracker create/update integration, reparenting, test plan
    - path: 07-phase6-blockages-spec.md
      description: Phase 6 specification — dependency-manager (bidirectional maps, cycle detection, batch atomicity, impact score), 4 Tracker blockage methods, auto-resolution on status change, CLI blockage commands, test plan
    - path: 08-phase7-polish-spec.md
      description: Phase 7 specification — JSDoc on all public API, error message polish, edge case & resilience testing, package configuration verification
    - path: next-command-spec.md
      description: "`next <user-name>` command — recommend highest-priority unblocked issue for a user. Returns ComputedIssue or NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE. Filters by assignee, actionable statuses (idea/todo/in-progress), no active blockages. Sort: priority ASC → impact DESC → id ASC."

access_boundaries:
  rule: "CTO must NOT read or modify files under packages/library/. All code changes go through subordinates."
  cto_scope: ".agentic/ files, root-level config, specifications, expertise files"
  code_access: "If code-level insight is needed, ask library-developer or library-quality to report — never read source directly."
  enforced: "2026-05-10 — user explicitly removed CTO read permissions on packages/library/"

delegation_workflow:
  mandatory_sequence: |
    After every library-developer delegation that makes code changes, you MUST delegate to library-quality for validation.
    Sequence: library-developer (implement) → library-quality (validate).
    Never skip the quality validation step. This ensures code is clean, tested, and documented.
  timeout_constraint: |
    Bash tool has a 10-minute (600000ms) timeout. Large phases must be split into sub-delegations of ~5-8 minutes each.
    Split strategy for Phase 3: (A) auth.ts + errors.ts, (B) user methods in tracker.ts, (C) auth integration into existing methods + CLI commands, (D) library-quality validation).
    Each sub-task should be scoped to a few files to stay within timeout.
  docs_ownership: |
    library-quality owns documentation tasks: TypeDoc configuration, doc generation, docs scripts.
    When docs need updating or generating, delegate directly to library-quality (not library-developer).
  access_gaps: |
    Resolved: user granted CTO access to .github/ folder. CTO now owns workflow files.
    Root .gitignore is also writable by CTO.
  note: "Self-check and validation steps are enforced via agent system prompts. No need to duplicate in delegation messages."
  library-developer:
    focus: "Focus delegation messages on the implementation task itself. The agent self-checks typecheck+lint+test before completion."
  library-quality:
    focus: "Always pass the same spec file used for implementation so quality can validate alignment with specs, not just code quality. Include the spec path, what changed, and what behaviors to validate. Also handles documentation tasks (TypeDoc, docs generation)."

docs_absorbed:
  - docs/introduction.md — project vision, file structure, issue properties, CLI overview, config
  - docs/hierarchy.md — parent-child tree, childrenOf map, status constraints, auto-promotion
  - docs/commands.md — full CLI reference: init, create, update, list, view, history, comments, blockages
  - docs/issue-blockages.md — dependency index, blockage lifecycle, cycle detection, impact scoring
  - docs/user-auth.md — token-based auth, user registry, auth modes (open/read-only/strict)

# CTO Expertise Index
# Agent: cto
# Domain: Project architecture, technical specifications, development alignment

hierarchy:
  manager: none — top-level agent
  subordinates:
    active:
      - library-developer (implementation)
      - library-quality (testing, documentation, quality gates)

expertise_status: "Dogfooding active. `next <user-name>` command implemented (mp3rc7femf, code complete). 569 tests, 1997 assertions, 100% tracker.ts line coverage. Active work: cap upward auto-promotion at in-progress (parent mp9xefucmo, in-progress). Both reviews done (mp9xemq9mv, mp9xemy1j3). Spec updated to REVIEWED with clarifications for reason strings, multi-level propagation, and 7 additional test cases. Implementation (mp9xevrbwj) now unblocked — runner should pick up. Validation (mp9xev4b6c) blocked by implementation. Pending issues: v0.1.0 release (mp3peanz8b), defensive index validation (mp3pe95w9e), typedoc-plugin-markdown cleanup (mp3pe9lvdq), concurrent writes decision (mp3pe96e9f), CLAUDE.md decision (mp3pe9urb9)."

project_overview:
  name: trackgentic
  type: npm library (TypeScript, Bun runtime)
  purpose: Issue tracker designed for AI agents — file-backed, event-sourced, git-friendly
  distribution: npm package with CLI entry point (`trackgentic` command)
  stage: Dogfooding active. Agent runner mechanism live and tested — CTO processed 3 `/worktask` invocations for test issue mp5r0cd19j, correctly following status-based workflow (idea → analyze+comment, no code changes). All pending work tracked as trackgentic issues. Current issue chain: cap upward auto-promotion at in-progress (mp9xefucmo, in-progress, 4 children). Standing issues: v0.1.0 release (mp3peanz8b), defensive index validation (mp3pe95w9e), typedoc-plugin-markdown cleanup (mp3pe9lvdq), concurrent writes decision (mp3pe96e9f), CLAUDE.md decision (mp3pe9urb9).
  bun_version: "1.3.11"
  build_tool: tsup (implemented, producing dist/)
  docs_tool: TypeDoc HTML output (configured, generates to docs-html/)

detail_files:
  - path: architecture.yaml
    description: Core data model, file structure, event sourcing, resolution algorithm, index design
  - path: design-decisions.yaml
    description: Architectural decisions and rationale (why file-backed, why event-sourced, etc.)
  - path: status.yaml
    description: Current project status, implementation roadmap, open questions, risks

specifications:
  location: .agentic/specs/
  files:
    - path: 01-overview.md
      description: Monorepo structure, library package layout, design principles, dependencies, CLI/API relationship
    - path: 02-data-model.md
      description: All TypeScript types, interfaces, file formats, initial file contents
    - path: 03-api-specification.md
      description: Every Tracker method with params, return types, behavior, error handling
    - path: 04-cli-specification.md
      description: Every CLI command with args, flags, auth, output format, error handling
    - path: 05-implementation-phases.md
      description: 7 phases from foundation to polish, with tasks, tests, and exit criteria per phase
    - path: 06-quality-plan.md
      description: Quality plan — now uses Prettier + ESLint (was Biome). Linting, typechecking (strict+), testing (Bun), test organization, documentation (TypeDoc), quality gates
    - path: 07-phase3-auth-users.md
      description: Phase 3 specification — auth module (resolveAuthor), user CRUD (register/list/revoke/regenerate), auth integration into existing methods, CLI user commands
    - path: 07-phase4-comments-spec.md
      description: Phase 4 specification — computeComments function, 4 Tracker comment methods, CLI comment commands, test plan
    - path: 08-phase5-hierarchy-spec.md
      description: Phase 5 specification — childrenOf map management, hierarchy.ts (upward/downward constraints), Tracker create/update integration, reparenting, test plan
    - path: 07-phase6-blockages-spec.md
      description: Phase 6 specification — dependency-manager (bidirectional maps, cycle detection, batch atomicity, impact score), 4 Tracker blockage methods, auto-resolution on status change, CLI blockage commands, test plan
    - path: 08-phase7-polish-spec.md
      description: Phase 7 specification — JSDoc on all public API, error message polish, edge case & resilience testing, package configuration verification
    - path: next-command-spec.md
      description: "`next <user-name>` command — recommend highest-priority unblocked issue for a user. Returns ComputedIssue or NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE. Filters by assignee, actionable statuses (idea/todo/in-progress), no active blockages. Sort: priority ASC → impact DESC → id ASC."

access_boundaries:
  rule: "CTO must NOT read or modify files under packages/library/. All code changes go through subordinates."
  cto_scope: ".agentic/ files, root-level config, specifications, expertise files"
  code_access: "If code-level insight is needed, ask library-developer or library-quality to report — never read source directly."
  enforced: "2026-05-10 — user explicitly removed CTO read permissions on packages/library/"

delegation_workflow:
  mandatory_sequence: |
    After every library-developer delegation that makes code changes, you MUST delegate to library-quality for validation.
    Sequence: library-developer (implement) → library-quality (validate).
    Never skip the quality validation step. This ensures code is clean, tested, and documented.
  timeout_constraint: |
    Bash tool has a 10-minute (600000ms) timeout. Large phases must be split into sub-delegations of ~5-8 minutes each.
    Split strategy for Phase 3: (A) auth.ts + errors.ts, (B) user methods in tracker.ts, (C) auth integration into existing methods + CLI commands, (D) library-quality validation).
    Each sub-task should be scoped to a few files to stay within timeout.
  docs_ownership: |
    library-quality owns documentation tasks: TypeDoc configuration, doc generation, docs scripts.
    When docs need updating or generating, delegate directly to library-quality (not library-developer).
  access_gaps: |
    Resolved: user granted CTO access to .github/ folder. CTO now owns workflow files.
    Root .gitignore is also writable by CTO.
  note: "Self-check and validation steps are enforced via agent system prompts. No need to duplicate in delegation messages."
  library-developer:
    focus: "Focus delegation messages on the implementation task itself. The agent self-checks typecheck+lint+test before completion."
  library-quality:
    focus: "Always pass the same spec file used for implementation so quality can validate alignment with specs, not just code quality. Include the spec path, what changed, and what behaviors to validate. Also handles documentation tasks (TypeDoc, docs generation)."

docs_absorbed:
  - docs/introduction.md — project vision, file structure, issue properties, CLI overview, config
  - docs/hierarchy.md — parent-child tree, childrenOf map, status constraints, auto-promotion
  - docs/commands.md — full CLI reference: init, create, update, list, view, history, comments, blockages
  - docs/issue-blockages.md — dependency index, blockage lifecycle, cycle detection, impact scoring
  - docs/user-auth.md — token-based auth, user registry, auth modes (open/read-only/strict)
